purpose: Classify BuildLang/buildc and adjacent Build ecosystem repositories by maturity, receipt capability, market lane, Telos integration point, and publication boundary.
claim_boundary: This registry is based on current local documentation reads. It is not a benchmark result, release certification, market claim, or proof that every repo has current passing CI.

evidence.summary: BuildLang docs identify buildc, the C backend as the primary verified execution path, experimental non-C backends, a shipped scientific-runtime receipt family, and explicit non-claims about physical laws or PDE correctness.
Build Universe docs identify an alpha, mixed-language ecosystem with canonical module maturity ledgers, self-hosting and whole-ecosystem compilation as goals, and tiered module reality.
Build app docs cover color science, display calibration, finance/backtesting, forecasting, adaptive paper-trading loops, shared PyQt UI, and the meta-package.
Editor docs identify syntax highlighting and grammar packages as editor support only, not compiler/runtime surfaces.

rows.name: BuildLang / buildc
rows.maturity_label: active compiler with shipped receipt lane and experimental surfaces
rows.receipt_capability: buildlang-scientific-runtime-receipt/v0 and buildlang-receipt-verification/v1 are documented surfaces.
verified_now: C backend is documented as the primary verified execution path.
Scientific runtime receipts are documented as shipped, with seven invariant families and paired negative fixtures.
Receipt verification is documented as re-running the program and re-checking source digest plus invariant verdict.
not_claimed: No claim that all non-C backends produce runnable artifacts.
No claim that linear types are fully sound.
No claim that self-hosting is achieved.
No claim that a scientific receipt proves a physical law, PDE correctness, or model validity.
rows.telos_integration: Primary compute substrate for Telos proof packets. Telos should import BuildLang receipt exports into Crucible assessments and research packets.
rows.publication_boundary: Methods-paper-ready only for accountable compute receipts; language replacement claims need separate benchmarks and adoption evidence.
next_actions: Create a Telos fixture that imports a buildlang-scientific-runtime-receipt/v0 row.
Add a Crucible assessment pack for one BuildLang positive and one negative scientific receipt.
Add a BuildLang typed DAG/QEC/color-kernel follow-on only after the import fixture is stable.

rows.name: Build Universe
rows.receipt_capability: Module maturity and organism checks are documented, but Telos receipt import is not yet implemented.
verified_now: STATUS.md is documented as the canonical module maturity ledger.
The README says self-hosting and whole-ecosystem cross-module compilation are goals, not achievements.
STATUS.md separates load-bearing modules, real kernels inside scaffolding, and sketch/showcase modules.
not_claimed: No claim that the ecosystem compiles as a whole.
No claim that BuildOS is production-ready.
No claim that all module code emits compilable native C.
rows.telos_integration: Use as the Build-family reality ledger and domain-module map, not as a single proof of shipped runtime capability.
rows.publication_boundary: Public docs can cite it as the Build family map only with alpha and module-maturity caveats.
next_actions: Import Build Universe module tiers into a Telos machine registry.
Mark Tier 3 modules as design intent unless a current receipt proves otherwise.
Tie Spectrum/Chromatic/Delta/Oracle lanes to proof demos only after BuildLang receipt replay exists.

rows.name: Build Color
rows.receipt_capability: No Telos receipt import yet; strong candidate for color/render measurement proof packets.
verified_now: README lists perceptual spaces, HDR tone mapping, color appearance models, Delta E metrics, chromatic adaptation, spectral utilities, and ICC profile generation.
ARCHITECTURE.md describes a numpy-only core with optional SciPy/Pillow/PyQt6 extras and deterministic offline operation.
not_claimed: No claim that generated profiles are device-measured without external measurement evidence.
No claim of universal color correctness.
rows.telos_integration: Pair with Calibrate Pro, Telos display calibration, and Crucible for a color/rendering measurement proof kit.
rows.publication_boundary: Proof-demo ready after profile hashes, transforms, reference inputs, and measured outputs are bound into receipts.
next_actions: Add a color transform receipt fixture with input/output hashes and Delta E calculation.
Compare against ACES/OCIO/Calman/ColourSpace in the market matrix.
Keep core color math separate from device calibration claims.

rows.name: Calibrate Pro
rows.maturity_label: public display-calibration tool with measurement caveats
rows.receipt_capability: README says it reaches host agents through the telos.display.calibration MCP contract; public Telos proof kit still needs a rechecked measurement packet.
verified_now: README documents CLI and GUI surfaces, panel database, DDC/CI, ICC/3D-LUT output, and CIEDE2000 verification.
README distinguishes sensorless estimates from measured i1Display3 accuracy.
not_claimed: No measured guarantee in sensorless mode.
No universal display accuracy claim.
Version text needs review because badge and current-status wording differ.
rows.telos_integration: Use as the device/display side of the color/rendering measurement proof kit.
rows.publication_boundary: Public claims need a device, profile, instrument, verification report, and drift boundary.
next_actions: Create a display-calibration proof-packet schema with device state, profile hash, instrument path, Delta E summary, and drift status.
Resolve README version drift before using version text in front-door docs.
Keep sensorless mode labeled as model estimate.

rows.name: Build Finance
rows.receipt_capability: No Telos receipt import yet; candidate for backtest and strategy proof packets.
verified_now: README documents strategies, indicators, risk metrics, backtesting, portfolio optimization, paper trading, and optional live broker execution.
SECURITY.md states paper trading is default, live execution is explicit opt-in, API keys are user-supplied, and the library custodies no funds.
not_claimed: No investment advice.
No expected return claim.
No live trading safety claim.
rows.telos_integration: Use as a finance proof-packet lane with data source, split rules, cost/slippage model, baseline, and non-advice gates.
rows.publication_boundary: Public demos must be paper/backtest-only unless a separate high-risk review approves live-mode language.
next_actions: Create a backtest receipt schema with data source, splits, costs, slippage, trial count, and drawdown.
Add Crucible negative controls for lookahead and missing-cost conditions.
Keep all public examples paper-mode and non-advisory.

rows.name: Build Oracle
rows.receipt_capability: No Telos receipt import yet; candidate for forecast-evaluation proof packets.
verified_now: README documents ARIMA, Prophet-style decomposition, neural forecasting, changepoint detection, feature engineering, CLI, and Python API.
not_claimed: No production forecasting accuracy claim.
No guarantee of model superiority.
rows.telos_integration: Pair with Model Foundry and Objective Monitor for forecast-eval receipts.
rows.publication_boundary: Public claims need dataset boundaries, baselines, horizon, error metrics, and leakage checks.
next_actions: Create forecast receipt schema with train/test boundary, horizon, metric, baseline, and leakage guard.
Connect Build Oracle outputs to Build Engine paper-mode loops.
Add Crucible drift checks for changed datasets or metric definitions.

rows.name: Build Engine
rows.maturity_label: public paper-first adaptive prediction and trading loop
rows.receipt_capability: No Telos receipt import yet; candidate for objective-monitor and loop-ledger proof packets.
verified_now: README says paper trading is default, live broker execution is explicit opt-in and gated, and the engine custodies no funds.
README describes a loop from market data to models, predictions, paper trades, results, and weights.
not_claimed: No financial advice.
No live execution readiness claim for public demos.
No guarantee that adaptive weights improve out-of-sample performance.
rows.telos_integration: Use as the quant instantiation of Telos Objective Monitor and loop ledger.
rows.publication_boundary: Public proof demos should stay in synthetic or paper mode with explicit trial-count and leakage boundaries.
next_actions: Create a paper-mode loop receipt with model config, prediction, simulated execution, outcome, weight change, and residual risk.
Add Objective Monitor signals for proxy/quality divergence.
Block live broker examples from public proof demos.

rows.name: Build UI
rows.receipt_capability: No proof receipt role yet; support layer for consistent operator surfaces.
verified_now: README documents shared PyQt6 theme constants, stylesheet generation, and reusable widgets.
not_claimed: No scientific or market proof claim.
No runtime verification claim.
rows.telos_integration: Use for Build-family UI consistency; keep separate from proof claims.
next_actions: Map common Build UI components to Telos app-shell needs if native UI packaging resumes.
Keep proof-kit claims in Build Color, Calibrate Pro, and Telos receipts instead.

rows.name: Build Ecosystem
verified_now: README identifies the meta-package as installing Build Color, Build Finance, Build Oracle, Build Engine, and Calibrate Pro.
not_claimed: No claim that the meta-package proves domain correctness.
No claim that BuildLang compiler ships inside this Python meta-package.
rows.telos_integration: Use for packaging and onboarding, not as the proof substrate.
next_actions: Ensure meta-package docs link to maturity boundaries for each included package.
Do not use meta-package existence as evidence that every included tool is production-ready.

rows.name: BuildLang VS Code Extension
rows.receipt_capability: No compiler or runtime receipt role.
verified_now: README states the extension provides syntax highlighting and language configuration and does not bundle or run the compiler.
not_claimed: No BuildLang compilation capability.
No browser automation execution despite example vocabulary.
rows.telos_integration: Developer-experience support for BuildLang proof-packet work.
next_actions: Keep examples aligned with current BuildLang syntax.
Do not treat editor fixture examples as executable proof demos.

rows.name: BuildLang TextMate Grammar
rows.receipt_capability: No compiler or runtime receipt role.
verified_now: README states this is a grammar package, not a compiler or runnable program.
not_claimed: No BuildLang compilation capability.
No runtime, backend, or proof-packet capability.
rows.telos_integration: Small support surface for readable BuildLang examples and demos.
next_actions: Keep grammar docs synchronized with BuildLang keyword and syntax changes.
Do not cite grammar coverage as compiler maturity evidence.

integration_priorities.name: BuildLang receipt import into Telos and Crucible
integration_priorities.reason: This is the strongest cross-domain bridge: it makes mathematical, physical, rendering, quant, and security kernels accountable through one receipt shape.
integration_priorities.first_artifact: Telos fixture consuming buildlang-scientific-runtime-receipt/v0 and emitting a Crucible measurement row.

integration_priorities.name: Color/rendering measurement proof kit
integration_priorities.reason: Build Color plus Calibrate Pro can turn visual output into measured proof packets rather than subjective screenshots.
integration_priorities.first_artifact: Profile/transform/device-state packet with Delta E and artifact hashes.

integration_priorities.name: Quant and forecast proof packets
integration_priorities.reason: Build Finance, Build Oracle, and Build Engine give a concrete high-stakes lane where receipts must block lookahead, live-risk overclaiming, and proxy-metric drift.
integration_priorities.first_artifact: Paper-mode forecast-to-backtest loop receipt with baseline, split, costs, and non-advice gate.

integration_priorities.name: Build Universe module ledger import
integration_priorities.reason: The module ecosystem is too broad for README-level truth; Telos should ingest the tier ledger and prevent Tier 3 sketches from becoming product claims.
integration_priorities.first_artifact: Machine row per Build Universe module with tier, score, reality, scaffolding, and Telos lane.

publication_controls: Use BuildLang/buildc for accountable compute claims only when a receipt, replay, and verifier outcome are present.
Use Build Universe as a domain map and maturity ledger, not as proof that every module is shipped.
Use Build Color and Calibrate Pro for color/rendering proof demos only after measured output, profile hashes, and device state are captured.
Use Build Finance, Build Oracle, and Build Engine for quant/forecasting papers only with data lineage, baselines, split rules, costs, and explicit non-advice gates.
Use Build UI, VS Code, and TextMate docs as support surfaces, not as evidence of compiler/runtime maturity.
next_actions: Create the Telos BuildLang receipt import fixture.
Create a Build Universe module-tier subregistry from STATUS.md.
Create a color/rendering measurement proof-kit schema joining Build Color and Calibrate Pro.
Create a finance/forecast paper-mode receipt schema joining Build Finance, Build Oracle, Build Engine, Objective Monitor, and loop ledger.
Resolve Calibrate Pro version wording drift before citing exact release version in public front-door docs.
